findings.description: The North Carolina state seal and DEQ logo in the HTML header area appear upside down/inverted compared to the PDF original.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the NC state seal right-side up at the top center and the DEQ logo right-side up at the bottom. HTML page 1 shows the state seal and 'NORTH CAROLINA Environmental Quality' text rendered upside down.

findings.description: The PDF letterhead layout has the officials' names (Roy Cooper, Michael S. Regan, Brian Wrenn) in a left column with the state seal centered at top. The HTML renders these elements in a single column with the seal/logo block appearing below the officials' names rather than beside them.
findings.evidence: PDF has a two-element header layout (names left, seal center). HTML renders everything in a single linear column flow.

findings.description: The signature image of David Miller is missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a handwritten signature between 'Sincerely,' and 'David Miller, PE'. HTML shows only the text without the signature image.

findings.description: The DEQ logo at the bottom of page 1 in the HTML footer area appears upside down/inverted.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the DEQ logo right-side up in the footer. HTML shows the DEQ logo upside down.

findings.description: The signatures of Brian Wrenn and Brenda Harris are missing from the HTML conversion of page 2.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows handwritten signatures for Brian Wrenn and Brenda Harris. HTML shows only their typed names without signature images.

findings.description: The h1 title 'Notice of Issuance of Mining Permit' is used as the document title, but the first page is actually a cover letter, not the notice itself. The notice title appears on page 2. The h1 effectively misrepresents the structure by making the letter appear to be part of the notice.
findings.evidence: Page 1 of PDF is a cover letter from David Miller dated December 15, 2020. The 'NOTICE OF ISSUANCE OF MINING PERMIT' heading appears on page 2 as a separate document. HTML uses this as h1 at the top, conflating the two documents.

findings.description: The HTML says 'November 30th' with the ordinal suffix 'th', but the PDF uses a superscripted 'th' after '30'. While the meaning is preserved, the HTML also has a comma after 'November 30th' that does not appear in the PDF text.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'was issued on November 30th, 2020.' HTML: 'was issued on November 30th, 2020.' — This is actually consistent; minor formatting difference only. Withdrawing this finding.

findings.description: The handwritten mailing date is transcribed as '12-15-2020' but the handwriting in the PDF reads '12-15-20 20' which appears to be '12-15-2020'. The transcription is reasonable.
